operatingSystems.commandQuoting: POSIX shell quoting; quote paths with single quotes when possible.
operatingSystems.symlinkBehavior: supported; may require permissions for protected locations
operatingSystems.permissionBehavior: POSIX permissions; executable bit matters for shell hooks
operatingSystems.browserAutomationBehavior: supported when browser dependencies are installed
operatingSystems.hookExecutionShell: host shell or configured command interpreter

operatingSystems.commandQuoting: Prefer PowerShell arrays/native cmdlets; quote with double quotes and avoid shell-built destructive paths.
operatingSystems.pythonCommand: py, python, or explicit LOCALAPPDATA Programs Python path; Windows Store shim is unsafe
operatingSystems.gitAvailability: required; Git for Windows commonly used
operatingSystems.symlinkBehavior: may require developer mode or elevation
operatingSystems.permissionBehavior: ACL-based; executable bit is not sufficient signal
operatingSystems.daemonServiceBehavior: PowerShell background process, Windows service, or scheduled task
operatingSystems.browserAutomationBehavior: supported with Windows browser paths; headless dependencies differ
operatingSystems.mcpServerStartup: stdio supported; long-running HTTP needs process supervision
operatingSystems.hookExecutionShell: PowerShell or command configured by runtime; script extensions matter

operatingSystems.commandQuoting: POSIX shell quoting; quote paths with single quotes when possible.
operatingSystems.symlinkBehavior: supported; executable bit and ownership matter
operatingSystems.permissionBehavior: POSIX permissions; executable bit matters for shell hooks
operatingSystems.daemonServiceBehavior: systemd when available or background shell process
operatingSystems.browserAutomationBehavior: supported when browser dependencies are installed
operatingSystems.hookExecutionShell: host shell or configured command interpreter

operatingSystems.commandQuoting: POSIX shell quoting; beware /mnt/c path boundaries.
operatingSystems.symlinkBehavior: Linux behavior inside distro; Windows mount behavior differs
operatingSystems.permissionBehavior: Linux permissions inside distro; Windows filesystem mounts may differ
operatingSystems.daemonServiceBehavior: systemd when enabled or background shell process
operatingSystems.browserAutomationBehavior: supported but may need Windows browser bridge or Linux browser install
operatingSystems.mcpServerStartup: stdio supported inside distro; Windows-host clients need path bridge
